topLevelSqlPolicy: Top-level SQL files are snapshots, manual ops scripts, diagnostics, or legacy patches. Deployable schema changes must be copied into a timestamped migration before production rollout.

files.notes: Configures pg_cron/pg_net/Vault for the driver reminder Edge Function.
Manual pg_net test for the driver-reminder Edge Function.
Targeted grant for refresh_driver_reminder_cron when needed.
Historical hard-delete patch. Move required changes into a new migration before reuse.
Historical notification hardening patch. Current DB history belongs in migrations.
Historical notification review patch. Move required changes into a new migration before reuse.
Historical combined security/cron patch. Do not run directly on migration-managed DBs.
Historical open-shifts patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
Historical push subscription hardening patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
Historical realtime publication patch. Move required changes into a migration before reuse.
Historical pre-migration RLS patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
Owner-role SQL probes that roll back their test data.
Configures pg_cron/pg_net/Vault for the daily scheduler Edge Function.
Manual pg_net test for the scheduler Edge Function.
Reference snapshot for orientation only. New projects should apply migrations.
Manual seed template after Auth users are created.
Historical availability refactor patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
Historical availability date patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
Historical settlement integration patch. Use migrations as source of truth.
